speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Your Lordship is the most patient man in

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: loss, the most coldest that ever turned up ace.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: It would make any man cold to lose.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: But not every man patient after the noble

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: temper of your Lordship. You are most hot and

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: furious when you win.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Winning will put any man into courage. If I

speaker: CLOTEN
text: could get this foolish Imogen, I should have gold

speaker: CLOTEN
text: enough. It’s almost morning, is ’t not?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Day, my lord.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I would this music would come. I am advised

speaker: CLOTEN
text: to give her music a-mornings; they say it will

speaker: CLOTEN
text: penetrate.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Come on, tune. If you can penetrate her with your

speaker: CLOTEN
text: fingering, so. We’ll try with tongue, too. If none

speaker: CLOTEN
text: will do, let her remain, but I’ll never give o’er. First,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: a very excellent good-conceited thing; after, a wonderful

speaker: CLOTEN
text: sweet air, with admirable rich words to it,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: and then let her consider.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Hark, hark, the lark at heaven’s gate sings,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And Phoebus gins arise,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: His steeds to water at those springs

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: On chaliced flowers that lies;

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: And winking Mary-buds begin

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: To ope their golden eyes.

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: With everything that pretty is,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: My lady sweet, arise,

speaker: UNKNOWN
text: Arise, arise.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: So, get you gone. If this penetrate, I will

speaker: CLOTEN
text: consider your music the better. If it do not, it is a

speaker: CLOTEN
text: vice in her ears which horsehairs and calves’

speaker: CLOTEN
text: guts, nor the voice of unpaved eunuch to boot, can

speaker: CLOTEN
text: never amend.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: Here comes the King.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I am glad I was up so late, for that’s the reason

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I was up so early. He cannot choose but take this

speaker: CLOTEN
text: service I have done fatherly.—Good morrow to

speaker: CLOTEN
text: your Majesty and to my gracious mother.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Attend you here the door of our stern daughter?

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Will she not forth?

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I have assailed her with musics, but she

speaker: CLOTEN
text: vouchsafes no notice.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: The exile of her minion is too new;

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: She hath not yet forgot him. Some more time

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Must wear the print of his remembrance on ’t,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: And then she’s yours.

speaker: QUEEN
text: You are most bound to th’ King,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Who lets go by no vantages that may

speaker: QUEEN
text: Prefer you to his daughter. Frame yourself

speaker: QUEEN
text: To orderly solicits and be friended

speaker: QUEEN
text: With aptness of the season. Make denials

speaker: QUEEN
text: Increase your services. So seem as if

speaker: QUEEN
text: You were inspired to do those duties which

speaker: QUEEN
text: You tender to her; that you in all obey her,

speaker: QUEEN
text: Save when command to your dismission tends,

speaker: QUEEN
text: And therein you are senseless.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Senseless? Not so.

speaker: MESSENGER
text: So like you, sir, ambassadors from Rome;

speaker: MESSENGER
text: The one is Caius Lucius.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: A worthy fellow,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Albeit he comes on angry purpose now.

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: But that’s no fault of his. We must receive him

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: According to the honor of his sender,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: And towards himself, his goodness forespent on us,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: We must extend our notice.—Our dear son,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: When you have given good morning to your mistress,

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: Attend the Queen and us. We shall have need

speaker: CYMBELINE
text: T’ employ you towards this Roman.—Come, our queen.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: If she be up, I’ll speak with her; if not,

speaker: CLOTEN
text:
Let her lie still and dream.
                 
                (
                He
                 
                knocks
                .
                )
               By your leave, ho!—

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I know her women are about her. What

speaker: CLOTEN
text: If I do line one of their hands? ’Tis gold

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Which buys admittance—oft it doth—yea, and makes

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Diana’s rangers false themselves, yield up

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Their deer to th’ stand o’ th’ stealer; and ’tis gold

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Which makes the true man killed and saves the thief,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Nay, sometime hangs both thief and true man. What

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Can it not do and undo? I will make

speaker: CLOTEN
text: One of her women lawyer to me, for

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I yet not understand the case myself.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: By your leave.

speaker: LADY
text: Who’s there that knocks?

speaker: CLOTEN
text: A gentleman.

speaker: LADY
text: No more?

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Yes, and a gentlewoman’s son.

speaker: LADY
text: That’s more

speaker: LADY
text: Than some whose tailors are as dear as yours

speaker: LADY
text: Can justly boast of. What’s your Lordship’s pleasure?

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Your lady’s person. Is she ready?

speaker: LADY
text: Ay,

speaker: LADY
text: To keep her chamber.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: There is gold for you.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Sell me your good report.

speaker: LADY
text: How, my good name? Or to report of you

speaker: LADY
text: What I shall think is good?

speaker: LADY
text: The Princess.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Good morrow, fairest sister. Your sweet hand.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Good morrow, sir. You lay out too much pains

speaker: IMOGEN
text: For purchasing but trouble. The thanks I give

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Is telling you that I am poor of thanks

speaker: IMOGEN
text: And scarce can spare them.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Still I swear I love you.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: If you but said so, ’twere as deep with me.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: If you swear still, your recompense is still

speaker: IMOGEN
text: That I regard it not.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: This is no answer.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: But that you shall not say I yield being silent,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I would not speak. I pray you, spare me. Faith,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I shall unfold equal discourtesy

speaker: IMOGEN
text: To your best kindness. One of your great knowing

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Should learn, being taught, forbearance.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: To leave you in your madness ’twere my sin.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I will not.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Fools are not mad folks.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Do you call me fool?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: As I am mad, I do.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: If you’ll be patient, I’ll no more be mad.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: That cures us both. I am much sorry, sir,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: You put me to forget a lady’s manners

speaker: IMOGEN
text: By being so verbal; and learn now for all

speaker: IMOGEN
text: That I, which know my heart, do here pronounce,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: By th’ very truth of it, I care not for you,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: And am so near the lack of charity

speaker: IMOGEN
text: To accuse myself I hate you—which I had rather

speaker: IMOGEN
text: You felt than make ’t my boast.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: You sin against

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Obedience, which you owe your father. For

speaker: CLOTEN
text: The contract you pretend with that base wretch—

speaker: CLOTEN
text: One bred of alms and fostered with cold dishes,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: With scraps o’ th’ court—it is no contract, none;

speaker: CLOTEN
text: And though it be allowed in meaner parties—

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Yet who than he more mean?—to knit their souls,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: On whom there is no more dependency

speaker: CLOTEN
text: But brats and beggary, in self-figured knot;

speaker: CLOTEN
text: Yet you are curbed from that enlargement by

speaker: CLOTEN
text: The consequence o’ th’ crown, and must not foil

speaker: CLOTEN
text: The precious note of it with a base slave,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: A hilding for a livery, a squire’s cloth,

speaker: CLOTEN
text: A pantler—not so eminent.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Profane fellow,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Wert thou the son of Jupiter and no more

speaker: IMOGEN
text: But what thou art besides, thou wert too base

speaker: IMOGEN
text: To be his groom. Thou wert dignified enough,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Even to the point of envy, if ’twere made

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Comparative for your virtues to be styled

speaker: IMOGEN
text: The under-hangman of his kingdom and hated

speaker: IMOGEN
text: For being preferred so well.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: The south fog rot him!

speaker: IMOGEN
text: He never can meet more mischance than come

speaker: IMOGEN
text: To be but named of thee. His mean’st garment

speaker: IMOGEN
text: That ever hath but clipped his body is dearer

speaker: IMOGEN
text: In my respect than all the hairs above thee,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Were they all made such men.—How now, Pisanio!

speaker: CLOTEN
text: “His garment”? Now the devil—

speaker: IMOGEN
text: To Dorothy, my woman, hie thee presently.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: “His garment”?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I am sprighted with a fool,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Frighted and angered worse. Go bid my woman

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Search for a jewel that too casually

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Hath left mine arm. It was thy master’s. Shrew me

speaker: IMOGEN
text: If I would lose it for a revenue

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Of any king’s in Europe. I do think

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I saw ’t this morning. Confident I am

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Last night ’twas on mine arm; I kissed it.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I hope it be not gone to tell my lord

speaker: IMOGEN
text: That I kiss aught but he.

speaker: PISANIO
text: ’Twill not be lost.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: I hope so. Go and search.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: You have abused me.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: “His meanest garment”?

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Ay, I said so, sir.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: If you will make ’t an action, call witness to ’t.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I will inform your father.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: Your mother too.

speaker: IMOGEN
text: She’s my good lady and will conceive, I hope,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: But the worst of me. So I leave you, sir,

speaker: IMOGEN
text: To th’ worst of discontent.

speaker: CLOTEN
text: I’ll be revenged! “His mean’st garment”? Well.
